paper_claims.claim: 55 UNSAT fence-sufficient + 40 SAT inherently observable Z3 certificates
642 meaningful differential tests + 3,000 determinism checks, all passing
75 patterns x 10 architecture configs = 750 test-model pairs

paper_claims.evidence: Literature-based validation: Owens/Sarkar/Sewell 2009, Pulte et al. 2018, Alglave et al. 2014, Sorensen & Donaldson 2016

paper_claims.claim: 6 critical + 5 warning GPU scope mismatch patterns

paper_claims.evidence: paper.tex Theorem 1 + Lemma 1 (RF×CO Decomposition). Proof by exhaustive enumeration. Machine-checked witness: 228/228 SMT agreement.
paper.tex Theorem 2. Trivially true by argmin construction. Paper explicitly acknowledges triviality.
paper.tex Theorem 3. Proof by analysis of ppo edge inclusion under scoped fence semantics.

paper_claims.claim: Minimal discriminating set of 2 patterns (isa2, mp_addr) covers 5/6 model pairs
5 discriminating tests synthesized, rediscovering MP and LB patterns
Custom models: SC 47 safe, POWER 27, Alpha 27, C11-relaxed 27 (of 57 patterns)
Model boundaries: TSO→PSO:6, PSO→ARM:7, ARM→RISC-V:1, GPU Dev→WG:5
mp_fence_wr is the sole ARM→RISC-V discriminator

paper_claims.evidence: Disclosed in paper. 228/228+108/108 SMT and 95 Z3 certificates provide machine-checked evidence.

paper_claims.claim: Per-category accuracy: kernel 87.5%/93.8%, synchronization 96.3%/96.3%, dependency 60%/100%, etc.

honesty_notes.herd7_validation: Validates against expected results from herd7's .cat specifications, NOT running herd7 directly. .litmus files provided for independent verification.
honesty_notes.hardware_validation: Consistency with published literature, not original hardware experiments.
honesty_notes.smt_encoding: Both enumerative and Z3-based encodings by same authors. Agreement demonstrates internal consistency, not external validation.
honesty_notes.fence_costs: Analytical weights reflecting ordering strength, not measured hardware latencies.
honesty_notes.gpu_models: 6 GPU configs are instantiations of 1 parameterized model. Independent model count is 5 (4 CPU + 1 GPU).
honesty_notes.mechanization: Theorems 1-3 are paper proofs, not mechanized in Coq/Isabelle/Lean.
honesty_notes.pattern_level: Pattern-level safety does not compose to program-level safety.
honesty_notes.theorem2_triviality: Theorem 2 is trivially true by construction (argmin is minimum).
honesty_notes.benchmark_self_generated: The 203-snippet benchmark was assembled by tool authors, not from an independent external corpus.
honesty_notes.false_negative_sample: 0/7 UNSAFE on self-generated benchmark; insufficient for safety-critical (10^-4) confidence levels.
honesty_notes.encoding_fidelity: Model implementations manually derived from specs, not mechanically extracted from .cat files. 228/228 herd7 agreement provides empirical but not formal fidelity evidence.
honesty_notes.gpu_approximation: GPU model is intentionally conservative; vendor-specific subtleties (Vulkan availability/visibility, PTX .weak) not modeled.

certificate_coverage_12.7%.critique: Certificate coverage at 12.7% (95/750) is too low
certificate_coverage_12.7%.response: The 95 Z3 certificates cover all 101 unsafe CPU pairs. Safe pairs are validated by exhaustive enumeration cross-checked by SMT (228/228 + 108/108) and herd7 (228/228). Discussion section now explicitly addresses this.

fixed_75_pattern_library.response: Acknowledged as fundamental design choice. AST analyzer bridges gap (96.6% accuracy). UnrecognizedPatternWarning added. Discussion explicitly states limitation.

no_formal_encoding_proof.critique: No formal proof encoding faithfully represents .cat specs
no_formal_encoding_proof.response: Acknowledged. 228/228 herd7 agreement provides empirical evidence. .litmus files provided for independent verification. Discussion addresses this.

gpu_model_approximation.response: Honestly stated as 1 parameterized model with 6 scope instantiations (not 10 independent). Discussion addresses API-specific gaps.

two_step_translation_fidelity.response: Acknowledged in validation methodology discussion. herd7 comparison provides one layer of external validation.

no_production_code_eval.response: Benchmark includes snippets from Linux kernel, Folly, crossbeam, DPDK, jemalloc, RocksDB, LevelDB, but these are extracted patterns, not full-program evaluation. Discussion acknowledges this.
